Name: Vredefort Crater
Notes: Largest known impact structure; likely caused by a 10–15 km asteroid

Name: Chicxulub Crater
Notes: Linked to the mass extinction event that wiped out the dinosaurs

Name: Sudbury Basin
Notes: One of Earth's oldest and largest impact structures; significant nickel and copper deposits

Name: Popigai Crater
Notes: Well-preserved crater; formed by a 5–8 km asteroid

Name: Manicouagan Crater
Notes: Visible from space; formed by a 5–10 km asteroid

Name: Acraman Crater
Notes: Evidence of early life; ejecta found over 300 km away

Name: Chesapeake Bay Crater
Notes: Submerged crater; formed by a 3 km asteroid

Name: Pingualuit Crater
Notes: Deep, pristine crater lake; formed by a 1–2 km asteroid

Name: Lonar Crater
Notes: Only known hyper-velocity impact crater in basaltic rock; formed by a 0.5–1 km asteroid

Name: Wolfe Creek Crater
Notes: One of the largest meteorite craters in the world; formed by a 4–5 m asteroid
